unit.name: Attention inside sequence-to-sequence RNNs
unit.description: Derives the original attention mechanism for encoder-decoder RNNs by computing alignment scores, softmax attention weights, and a fresh context vector at every decoder step.
unit.summary: To remove the single-vector bottleneck, the decoder is allowed to look back at the full encoder sequence at every output step [ts=540s][ts=548s]. For each decoder state, the model computes scalar alignment scores against every encoder hidden state, normalizes them with softmax into attention weights, and then forms a context vector as a weighted sum of encoder states [ts=590s][ts=660s][ts=738s]. This context vector is concatenated into the decoder computation so the decoder can focus on the most relevant input tokens for the current output token, such as attending more to “we see” when predicting the Italian word corresponding to that phrase [ts=816s][ts=856s]. The whole mechanism is differentiable and learned end-to-end with the standard output loss, so the model does not need explicit supervision of alignments [ts=882s][ts=900s]. Repeating this process at every decoder time step produces a different context vector each time and removes the original fixed-context bottleneck [ts=1080s][ts=1147s].

key_points.text: Alignment scores compare the decoder state to each encoder state: e_{t,i} = f_att(s_{t-1}, h_i).
Softmax converts alignment scores into attention weights that form a probability distribution over input positions.
The context vector is a weighted sum of encoder states: c_t = sum_i a_{t,i} h_i.
When predicting the Italian word for “we see,” the model should place larger weights on the corresponding English tokens than on “the sky.”
Attention weights are learned without direct supervision because the entire computation is differentiable and trained end-to-end.
A new context vector is recomputed at each decoder step instead of reusing one fixed summary vector for the whole output sequence.

kp_catalog.name: Attention scoring and alignment weights
kp_catalog.description: Attention begins by comparing a decoder/query state with each candidate source state, then applying softmax to turn those scores into normalized attention weights. Attention scores can be computed with dot-product, bilinear/multiplicative, low-rank projected dot-product, or additive neural-net scoring.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because attention scoring and alignment weights clarifies attention begins by comparing a decoder/query state with each candidate source state, then applying softmax to turn those scores into normalized attention weights, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices though importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: Context vector as a weighted sum
kp_catalog.description: The output of attention is a context vector formed by weighting source hidden states with attention weights and summing them.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because context vector as a weighted sum clarifies the output of attention is a context vector formed by weighting source hidden states with attention weights and summing them, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Seq2seq bottleneck motivation for attention
kp_catalog.description: Encoder-decoder RNNs compress the input sequence into one context vector, which limits performance on longer sequences because all input-output communication must pass through that fixed-size representation. A single encoder hidden state is too narrow a bottleneck for long source sentences, motivating selective access to all encoder states during decoding.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because seq2seq bottleneck motivation for attention clarifies encoder-decoder RNNs compress the input sequence into one context vector, which limits performance on longer sequences because all input-output communication must pass through that fixed-size representation, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
